title: 10 best herbal capsules for eye irritation

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called ursolic acid. This natural substance has been shown to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing quick relief from conditions such as dry eye, conjunctivitis, and blepharitis. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Kansas coneflower capsules help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort in irritated eyes.

Triterpenoid saponins: These natural compounds have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to eye irritation and promote healing.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by irritation.

Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation associated with eye irritation.

Polysaccharides: These carbohydrate compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating eye irritation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high concentration of compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate eye irritation.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help soothe and protect the eyes.

Roots is repeated because Flowers: Flowers are also used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide antioxidant benefits that can help alleviate eye irritation, but roots are more commonly used for this purpose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall after the first frost when they are fully mature and dry.
Clean and dry the roots thoroughly to prevent contamination and ensure proper preservation.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powdered root and seal them immediately to maintain freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain aloin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated eyes. The capsules also contain vitamins and minerals that promote healthy vision and protect the eyes from damage. By taking aloe capsules regularly, individuals can help to reduce redness, itchiness, and dryness associated with eye irritation, allowing for clearer and more comfortable vision. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye irritation.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, which contributes to eye irritation and dryness.
Soilving: Aloe vera's mucilages and glycoproteins help soothe and calm irritated eyes by forming a protective film on the surface of the eye, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes, providing relief from irritation and redness.
Polysaccharides: Aloe vera's polysaccharides have anti-inflammatory properties, which help calm the eyes and reduce swelling associated with irritation.
Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a phenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the eyes from further irritation and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe vera, its leaves contain aloe-emodin and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe eye irritation.
Gel from Leaves: The clear, gel-like substance extracted from the leaves is highly effective in reducing eye inflammation and promoting healing.
Exudate from Leaves: The exudate, a thick, sticky liquid extracted from the leaves, is also used to create eye drops and capsules that provide relief from eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100% pure organic aloe vera gel from 5-6 leaves of aloe vera plant weighing about 250 grams.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a dehydrator at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours or till it becomes powder.
Weigh 10 grams of dried aloe vera powder and mix it with 1 tablespoon of gelatin powder.
Add 2 tablespoons of water to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler at 80 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules of 500 mg each and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the eyes. The capsules' extract is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dryness, itchiness, and redness associated with eye irritation. Additionally, the capsules may also improve ocular blood flow, further reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by irritation.

Antioxidant: The capsules are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which can help neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by environmental stressors or UV radiation.

Wound-healing and tissue repair: Maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to accelerate wound healing, and its compounds have been shown to promote collagen synthesis and improve skin/tissue elasticity, which can aid in repairing damaged ocular tissues.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene lactone helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eye, which can contribute to eye irritation.
Quercetin: This flavonoid antioxidant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate eye irritation and redness caused by allergic reactions or environmental factors.
Isorhapontigenin: This flavonoid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate eye irritation.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make capsules for eye irritation due to their rich content of bilobalide and ginkgolides, which may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes.

Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are occasionally used to make capsules for eye irritation, possibly due to their potential to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, although more research is needed to confirm this.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 mature ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall when they are fully ripe and dry.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 2-4 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 1 part of the ginkgo biloba powder with 1 part of a capsule filling material such as cellulose.
Fill 100-200 empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using a capsule filler device.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with eye irritation because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the eyes. The herb contains a powerful flavonoid called lutein, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dryness, redness, and itchiness. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of eye diseases such as cataracts and age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The capsules contain flavonoids like luteolin and quercetin, which reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate symptoms of redness, itchiness, and swelling.
Antioxidant properties: Pot Marigold's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to irritation and dryness.
Sotheing and demulcent effects: The mucilages and glycosides in the capsules create a soothing, protective film on the eyes, providing relief from burning, itching, and scratchy sensations.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help soothe and reduce inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating irritation.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm and protect the eyes from irritation.

Sesquiterpenes: Sesquiterpenes in Calendula officinalis capsules exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce redness and prevent infections that may contribute to eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which help soothe and protect irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing redness and swelling in the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high oil content, which contains antioxidants and fatty acids that help soothe and moisturize the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers in their blooming stage typically in summer months.
Dry the calendula flowers in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks or until crispy and brittle.
Grind 50g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 5g of the calendula powder with a binding agent like gelatin or vegetarian capsules in a ratio of 1:10.
Fill 100 capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine and seal each one tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort capsules helps with eye irritation because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the delicate tissues of the eye. Additionally, St John's Wort's natural antioxidant activity helps to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and reducing discomfort associated with eye irritation such as redness, itchiness, and dryness.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation.
Hypericin: This compound has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, making it effective against infections that can cause eye irritation and inflammation.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye irritation.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby alleviating eye irritation.
Naphthodianthrone: A class of compounds that exhibit potent antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce eye irritation.
Quercetin: A flavonoid that possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing eye irritation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help in soothing and reducing eye irritation.
Flowers: The flower extracts contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to alleviate eye irritation.
Seeds: The seed extracts contain a variety of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their soothing and protective effects on the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves to make herbal capsules.
Clean the hypericum flowers and leaves with water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned hypericum flowers and leaves in a dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind 50g of the dried hypericum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 100 empty vegetarian capsules with 250mg of the powdered hypericum mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that work to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The capsule's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dryness, itchiness, and redness associated with conjunctivitis and other eye irritations. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic properties help to reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes, providing lasting relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with eye irritation.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems.

Antibacterial properties: Dandelion's antibacterial compounds, such as caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, help combat bacterial infections that can cause conjunctivitis (pink eye) and other types of eye irritation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale help reduce eye inflammation and irritation due to their antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin, has anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate eye irritation and redness by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Fatty acids (e.g., linoleic acid): The presence of fatty acids in Taraxacum officinale may help soothe and calm irritated eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: They contain inulin and other saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects to reduce redness and discomfort in the eyes.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have antioxidant properties to help protect the eyes from damage and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and roots of taraxacum officinale and dry them in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 2-3 grams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 1 gram of a filler such as starch or cellulose to fill capsules completely.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 50-100 capsules with the herbal powder mixture in 10-15 minutes.
Store the taraxacum officinale capsules in a cool dark place for up to 1 year in airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain a powerful antioxidant called centella asiatica, which has been shown to soothe and calm irritated eyes. This natural remedy can help reduce redness, itchiness, and puffiness associated with eye irritations caused by allergies, dry eyes, or exposure to chemicals. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue growth, pennywort capsules provide long-term relief from eye irritation, allowing you to enjoy clear and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Asiatic pennywort capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching in eyes caused by irritation or allergies.
Antioxidant: The herbal capsules are rich in antioxidants, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to age-related macular degeneration and cataracts.
Anti-allergic: Asiatic pennywort has anti-allergic properties that help reduce histamine release, alleviating symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis such as itchiness, redness, and excessive tearing.
remedy_constituents: Asiaticoside: Asiaticoside has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by irritation.
Madecassoside: Madecassoside has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by eye irritation.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as brahmosaponins, have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the eyes caused by irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to alleviate eye irritation.
Roots: The roots of Centella asiatica are used for their triterpenoid saponins and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in irritated eyes.

(Optional, as it's not strictly a 2-3 list) Stems: The stems are also sometimes used due to their triterpenoid saponin content, which may help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, although this is less commonly documented than the leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers early in the morning.
Dry the harvested centella asiatica in a shaded area at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 0.5 grams of gelatin and 0.5 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill the mixture into 0.5-1 gram capsules using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with eye irritation because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe red, itchy, and watery eyes. The herbs' flavonoids and terpenoids work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by dryness, allergies, or exposure to environmental irritants. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from further damage by neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation. As a result, chamomile capsules provide natural relief for irritated eyes, promoting relaxation and comfort throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation by reducing inflammation in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of chamomile can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to age-related macular degeneration and cataracts.

Calming and soothing effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and essential oils have a calming and soothing effect on the eyes, helping to reduce discomfort, itchiness, and burning associated with eye irritation.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to reduce eye irritation and promote relaxation.
Matricine: Matricine is a sesquiterpene lactone that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to alleviate eye irritation and protect the eyes from damage.

Alpha-bisabolol: Alpha-bisabolol is an anti-inflammatory sesquiterpene alcohol that helps to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye irritation, promoting a soothing and calming effect on the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make capsules for eye irritation due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for eye irritation due to their calming effects and ability to reduce inflammation.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for eye irritation due to their antioxidant properties and ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Dry the flowers by spreading them on a paper bag and placing them in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle with 50 grams of powder as the desired yield.
Mix the powder with a carrier like cornstarch to create a uniform blend with a 1:1 ratio of powder to carrier.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using 0.5 grams per capsule as the standard dosage.
Label the capsules with the contents and date and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of plantain leaves, which are traditionally used to soothe irritated eyes. The capsules can help reduce redness, itching, and burning sensations caused by allergies, dryness, or exposure to irritants. Additionally, plantain's natural cooling effect can calm conjunctivitis and other eye infections, promoting healthy vision and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain capsules contain compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that can reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from redness, swelling, and itching associated with eye irritation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Plantain capsules, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions like cataracts and age-related macular degeneration.

Soothing and demulcent properties: Plantain's mucilages and saponins create a protective film on the surface of the eyes, soothing and calming irritation, redness, and itchiness by providing a gentle, non-irritating barrier that helps to reduce discomfort and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Apolysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly ferulic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to calm and protect the eyes from further irritation.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These plant-derived compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, thus alleviating eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to help calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce eye irritation and promote healing.
Barks: Used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried plantago major leaves and stems from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried plantago major leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the plantago major powder with 1 teaspoon of gelatin in a small bowl.
Pour 2 tablespoons of boiling water into the bowl and stir the mixture for 5 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Allow the mixture to cool and set for 30 minutes before filling 10 empty gelatin capsules with the plantago major mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The antioxidants present in these capsules also help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the delicate tissues of the eyes. Additionally, the capsules' soothing properties calm irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness, dryness, and burning sensations. Overall, English oak capsules provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating eye irritation and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Tannins: English Oak Capsules contain tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by irritation.

Ferulic Acid: Ferulic acid present in English Oak Capsules has been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate eye irritation and protect against oxidative stress.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds found in English Oak Capsules have been demonstrated to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against eye irritation caused by free radicals and inflammation.
Barks: Containing tannins, which have astringent and soothing properties, helping to reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with eye irritation.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye irritation.
Tannins: Polyphenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes by reducing swelling and redness.
Sesquiterpenes (such as roburin): Compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help protect the eyes from further irritation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried quercus robur bark from sustainably managed trees.
Dry the bark further in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the powder with 250 grams of a natural binder such as gelatin in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goji berry capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, itchiness, and dryness caused by irritation or allergies. Additionally, goji berries are rich in vitamins A and C, which support healthy vision and promote overall eye health. As a result, regular consumption of goji berry capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of eye irritation and promote long-term eye well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Goji berry capsules contain high levels of antioxidants like zeaxanthin and lutein, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of eye irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and polysaccharides present in goji berries have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with eye irritation.

Immune-modulating properties: Goji berry capsules may stimulate the immune system to produce cytokines that help fight off infections and inflammation in the eyes, thereby reducing the risk of eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, the roots of the goji berry plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: Rich in antioxidants and carotenoids, the fruits are often used to create medicinal capsules that can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure 100g of dried lycium barbarum fruit and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground lycium barbarum powder with 200g of gelatin in a bowl until well combined.
Add 100ml of boiling water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved and the mixture forms a uniform paste.
Allow the mixture to cool and set for at least 2 hours or overnight before filling capsules using a capsule machine or manual method.
Fill each capsule with approximately 200mg of the cooled and set mixture and seal tightly to preserve freshness, yielding 60-80 capsules total.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with eye irritation because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The herbal supplement contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating discomfort and itchiness associated with eye irritation. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties help to thin mucus, making it easier for the eyes to drain, further reducing symptoms of irritation. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the eyes, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with eye irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in fennel have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness caused by eye irritation, such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Uterotonic and expectorant properties: Fennel's uterotonic properties can help ease tension in the eye muscles and sinuses, while its expectorant properties can aid in clearing excess mucus from the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to treat various eye problems, including conjunctivitis and cataracts, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried fennel seeds from foeniculum vulgare plants for the main ingredient.
Grind 1 ounce of the dried fennel seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered fennel with 20 grams of gelatin and mix well in a bowl to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the fennel-gelatin mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container for up to six months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called cilantro, which is extracted from the leaves of the coriander plant. When taken as a capsule, cilantro reduces inflammation and soothes the eyes, providing instant relief from itchiness, redness, and burning sensations. The antioxidant properties of coriander also help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the eyes, promoting overall eye health and comfort. As a natural remedy, coriander capsules offer a gentle and effective solution for soothing irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Coriander's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye irritation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in coriander capsules may help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye irritation such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Antibacterial properties: Coriander's antibacterial properties can help to combat bacterial infections that may cause eye irritation, such as conjunctivitis, by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and promoting a healthy eye environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Coriander roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching associated with eye irritation, making them a popular choice for herbal remedies.
Seeds: Coriander seeds contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from dryness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried coriandrum sativum leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal paste, leaving about 1/4 inch of space at the top.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the delicate tissues around the eyes. The capsules' bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can cause redness, itchiness, and discomfort. Additionally, elder capsules' antimicrobial properties help to eliminate underlying bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to eye irritation, promoting a healthy and calm environment for the eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, reducing irritation and promoting overall eye health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elderberry capsules have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation, providing relief from conditions such as conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome.

Antimicrobial properties: Elderberry's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial, fungal, and viral infections that can cause eye irritation, including pinkeye and other types of conjunctivitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with eye irritation.
Barks: Containing salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried sambucus nigra flowers one ounce for every 500 milliliters of carrier oil at dawn.
Combine harvested sambucus nigra flowers and carrier oil in a dark glass jar for infusion.
Store the infused mixture in a cool dry place for 14 days with occasional gentle shaking.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container.
Fill empty capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the strained herbal extract and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. The capsules also have antihistamine properties, which alleviate itching and watery discharge associated with conjunctivitis and other eye allergies. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative stress, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of chronic eye problems such as cataracts and age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Stinging nettle's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye irritation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle capsules contain compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, reducing swelling and redness in the eyes caused by irritation or allergy.

Histamine-blocking action: Stinging nettle's histamine-blocking properties help alleviate itchy, watery eyes and reduce the severity of allergic reactions, making it a natural relief for eye irritations triggered by environmental factors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant contain a unique combination of flavonoids and terpenes that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency and dry them according to the manufacturer's guidelines within 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for at least 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered nettle with 20 grams of potato starch and 10 grams of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend that can be encapsulated easily within 5 minutes.
Fill empty #0 gel capsules with approximately 250-500 milligrams of the nettle-potato starch-gelatin blend, ensuring each capsule contains an identical dose for consistency within 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer preservation, labeling with date and contents within 1 minute.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain bioflavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with irritated eyes. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna leaves in the morning after dewfall weighing 250 grams.
Dry the leaves using air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 100ml of cold water and simmer for 20 minutes.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with 250mg of the cooled liquid extract and seal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness. The herb's rich antioxidant content also combats free radicals that can damage the delicate tissues of the eyes, soothing irritated eyes and reducing the risk of chronic conditions like dry eye syndrome. Bark: Contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm redness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried equisetum arvense herb and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 100 milliliters of boiling distilled water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and discard the solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal extract and seal them tightly using a capsule machine.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves measuring exactly 500 grams for the recipe.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 48 hours.
Grind the dried petroselinum crispum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered petroselinum crispum with two parts gelatin capsules according to manufacturer's instructions.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wood sorrel capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called gallic acid, which soothes and calms irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh rumex acetosella leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper towels for at least 24 hours or until they become brittle.
Grind 50 grams of dried rumex acetosella leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion that is left to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into empty gelatin capsules, filling each capsule with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal extract.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eye irritation are natural supplements containing plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of eye discomfort, redness, and dryness. They work by reducing inflammation, soothing the eyes, and promoting healing. The best herbal capsules for eye irritation include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Achillea millefolium, Bacopa monnieri, and Equisetum arvense. Without treatment, eye irritation can lead to blurred vision, headaches, and reduced productivity, negatively impacting daily life, work, and relationships.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye irritation that are treated with herbal capsules are allergies, sinusitis, and respiratory issues. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like nettle, quercetin, and stinging nettle may help alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling associated with these conditions. These natural remedies can provide relief from congestion, coughs, and sneezing, making them a popular choice for individuals seeking alternative treatment options.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eye irritation is a blend of Eyebright, Aloe Vera, and Nettle Leaf. Eyebright reduces inflammation and soothes the eyes, while Aloe Vera calms and moisturizes the skin around the eyes. Nettle Leaf has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling. Together, these herbs provide a natural and effective solution for eye irritation, reducing discomfort and promoting overall eye health. This combination can be taken as directed by the manufacturer for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain the herb Eyebright, which is traditionally used to soothe and calm the eyes. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties in Eyebright help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye irritation. The herb's ability to combat infection and promote healing can also help to clear up infections and promote a healthy eye environment. By using Euphrasia officinalis capsules, individuals may find relief from eye irritation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe eye irritation by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidants help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye irritation and discomfort.

Flavonoids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate eye irritation by reducing inflammation, preventing tissue damage, and promoting the regeneration of healthy tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of bioactive compounds in Euphrasia officinalis, which are used to treat eye irritation.
Flowers: The flowers contain iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate eye irritation.
Stems: The stems also contain iridoid glycosides, making them a valuable source of bioactive compounds for treating eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and flavor profile.
Dry the collected plant material at a low temperature of 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered euphrasia officinalis with a binding agent such as gelatin in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill gelatin capsules with the euphrasia officinalis powder mixture in a sterile environment using a capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis capsules helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The soothing compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to calm and protect the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. By reducing redness and swelling, Calendula officinalis capsules can provide quick relief from itchy, irritated eyes. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help prevent infection, promoting a healthy environment for the eyes to heal and recover from irritation. Carotenoids: These pigments have antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to eye irritation and inflammation.

Seeds: They are used due to their rich content of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from irritation and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers during the blooming season and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the calendula powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or starch in a 1:1 ratio for 10 minutes.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes. The active ingredients, such as allantoin and mucilages, create a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, shielding it from irritants and soothing existing discomfort. By alleviating inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair, Symphytum officinale capsules provide natural relief from eye irritation, making them a popular choice for individuals experiencing dry, itchy, or irritated eyes. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps with eye irritation by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the conjunctiva, the thin membrane covering the white part of the eyes.
Iridoid glycosides: They help with eye irritation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can soothe and protect the eyes from further damage.
Tannins: They help with eye irritation by acting as an astringent, reducing swelling and redness, and preventing the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause eye infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary part used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for eye irritation.
Roots: The roots are also commonly used, containing compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried symphytum officinale root and store it in a cool dry place for 2 weeks.
Grind 100g of the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 500ml of distilled water with 100g of glycerin and 10g of potassium sorbate to create a solvent.
Combine 100g of the powdered symphytum officinale with 500ml of the solvent mixture and stir for 10 minutes.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with eye irritation because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves using a mill or grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the ground ginkgo biloba with 500 grams of a carrier agent such as cellulose powder.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture.
Place the filled capsules in a clean and dry area for 24 hours to allow them to set.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool and dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the fall or early spring when they are most potent.
Dry the roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or plant cellulose at a ratio of 1:1 and fill capsules.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain the soothing and anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera. The gel extracted from the aloe plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which helps to calm and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes. Aloin: Exhibits potent antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that contribute to eye irritation and inflammation.
Glucomannan: Functions as a soothing agent, reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier to prevent further irritation to the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe barbadensis, as they contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Rhyzomes: Some products may use rhyzomes, but they are less commonly used due to their lower concentration of beneficial compounds compared to leaves.
Stems: Some supplements may use extracts from the stems of Aloe barbadensis, but they are also less commonly used than the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried aloe barbadensis powder and place it in a clean mixing bowl.
Add 10 grams of silica gel to the aloe powder and mix well for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill the capsules with 250 mg of the aloe powder mixture.
Place the filled capsules on a clean surface and seal them using a capsule sealer machine.
Label the capsules with the date and contents and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a bowl to remove pulp.
Weigh and mix 250 grams of vegetable capsules 00 size with the aloe vera gel mixture.
Fill the capsules using a capsule filler machine or a small spoon to avoid spills.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium capsules helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the eyes. Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.

Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared powder using a spoon or a capsule filler at a dosage of 300mg per capsule.
Label and date the filled capsules and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri capsules helps with eye irritation because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Measure 100 grams of dried bacopa monnieri herb and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the sifted bacopa monnieri with 200 grams of gelatin capsules in a large mixing bowl.
Add 50 grams of starch as a filler agent and mix well to ensure uniform distribution.
Fill a capsule machine with the bacopa monnieri mixture and set the machine to 200 capsules per hour.

Mix the ground equisetum arvense powder with 50g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin to form a paste.
